---
title: "physmerge Demonstration"
author: "Pin"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r Library_and_Toy_dataset}
#devtools::install_github("Droideight/physmerge", force = TRUE)
set.seed(42)
n <- 500
pos <- sort(sample(1:5e6, n))
pval <- runif(n, 0.01, 1)

# Locus 1: clean single peak
peak1 <- which.min(abs(pos - 1050000))
pval[peak1 + c(-3,-2,-1,0,1,2,3)] <- c(1e-5, 1e-6, 1e-7, 1e-10, 1e-8, 1e-6, 1e-5)

# Locus 2: clean single peak
peak2 <- which.min(abs(pos - 2500000))
pval[peak2 + c(-2,-1,0,1,2)] <- c(1e-6, 1e-7, 3e-9, 1e-7, 1e-6)

# Locus 3: two strong peaks bridged by a weak significant SNP
#   best → two blocks (bridge does not reset steps, window exhausts between peaks)
#   any  → one block  (bridge resets steps, extending into the second peak)
peak3 <- which.min(abs(pos - 4000000))
pval[peak3 + c(-1, 0, 1)] <- c(1e-6, 1e-9, 1e-6)   # strong peak A
bridge <- which.min(abs(pos - 4350000))
pval[bridge]               <- 3e-8                    # weak bridge SNP (~350kb from A)
peak4 <- which.min(abs(pos - 4700000))
pval[peak4 + c(-1, 0, 1)] <- c(1e-6, 2e-9, 1e-6)   # strong peak B (~700kb from A)

toy_df <- data.frame(position = pos, value = pval)
```

```{r Run_with_preprocess_DF}
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Physical Merge — default (reset_on = "best")
# Window resets only when a more significant SNP is found.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
merged_best <- physical_merge(
  toy_df,
  5e-8,
  500000,
  reward   = "min",
  reset_on = "best"   # default; equivalent to omitting the argument
)
merged_best

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Physical Merge — reset_on = "any" (locusDefiner-equivalent)
# Window resets on every significant SNP, regardless of strength.
# Equivalent to taking the union of ±window intervals around each sig SNP.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
merged_any <- physical_merge(
  toy_df,
  5e-8,
  500000,
  reward   = "min",
  reset_on = "any"
)
merged_any

# Compare: blocks that differ between the two modes are edge cases where a
# weak bridging SNP connects two strong signals.

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Annotate Blocks
# This function joins the SNP information back with the original dataset.
# Physical merge tells you: Block 1 rep: SNP xyz, P val = p.
# This function joins the Ref, Alt, Coefficient... information back.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
recovered_toy_df <- annotate_blocks(
  merged_toy_df,     # output from physical_merge()
  toy_df,            # original input data frame (must have `position` column)
  #chrom_col = NULL,  # chromosome column name; auto-detects CHROM / #CHROM
  #id_col    = NULL   # SNP ID column name; auto-detects ID / SNP
)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
head(recovered_toy_df)
# They are the same because our toy dataset does not load additional info, see
# Plink cases below.
```

```{r Run_with_Plink_file}
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Read Sumstat
# This function takes the output from plink, etc and convert to 2-col df.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
plink <- read_sumstat(
  "/Users/user/Desktop/Classes/Res Prog/Housekeeping/TE_One_CH22.plink2.HbA1c.glm.linear.assoc.txt", 
  # path, change to a sumstat you have to test!
  format      = "plink2",   # "plink2" / "gpcm" / "custom"
  #value_col   = NULL,      # value column name (no need to fill in standard GWAS)
  #chrom_col   = NULL,      # chromosome column name (no need to fill in standard GWAS)
  #pos_col     = NULL,      # position column name (no need to fill in standard GWAS)
  #id_col      = NULL,      # SNP ID column name (no need to fill in standard GWAS)
  test_filter = TRUE,      # TRUE/FALSE; whether to filter by TEST column
  test_col    = "TEST",    # name of the TEST column
  test_val    = "ADD",     # value to retain in TEST column
  chrom       = NULL       # 1:22 (if didn't fill, take all)
)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
head(plink$data)
merged_plink <- physical_merge(plink$data, 5e-8, 500000, reward = "min")
head(merged_plink)
recovered_plink <- annotate_blocks(merged_plink, plink$data)
head(recovered_plink)
# You can see annotate block now produces different result.
# "plink" has two field: data and reward. To access, plink$data is necessary.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Export SNP List
# This function produces the SNP list needed to produce RAW in swiss army knife.
# Can produce one file or by chromosome.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
export_snp_list(
  recovered_plink,   # output from annotate_blocks()
  "/Users/user/Desktop/Classes/Res Prog/Housekeeping/snp_ids.txt",  
  # output path (.txt or .zip)
  by_chrom = FALSE   # FALSE = single .txt; TRUE = per-chromosome .zip
  #id_col   = NULL   # ID column name; auto-detects rps_ID / rps_BP
)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
ids_check <- readLines("/Users/user/Desktop/Classes/Res Prog/Housekeeping/snp_ids.txt")
cat("Total IDs:", length(ids_check), "\n")
head(ids_check)
```

```{r Run_with_GPCM_file}
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
gpcm <- read_sumstat("/Users/user/Desktop/Classes/Res Prog/Housekeeping/stage1_ch1_preclump.csv", 
  format = "gpcm", test_filter = FALSE)
head(gpcm$data)
merged_gpcm <- physical_merge(gpcm$data, 5e-8, 500000, reward = "min")
head(merged_gpcm)
recovered_gpcm <- annotate_blocks(merged_gpcm, gpcm$data)
head(recovered_gpcm)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
export_snp_list(recovered_gpcm, 
  "/Users/user/Desktop/Classes/Res Prog/Housekeeping/snp_id_gpcm.txt",  
  by_chrom = FALSE)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
ids_checkz <- readLines("/Users/user/Desktop/Classes/Res Prog/Housekeeping/snp_id_gpcm.txt")
cat("Total IDs:", length(ids_checkz), "\n")
head(ids_checkz)
```

```{r Run_with_cross_chr}
# ── Multi-chromosome toy dataset for physmerge testing ──────────────────────────
devtools::install_github("Droideight/physmerge", force = TRUE)
library(physmerge)

set.seed(42)

make_chr <- function(chr_label, n, max_bp, peaks) {
  pos  <- sort(sample(seq_len(max_bp), n))
  pval <- runif(n, 0.01, 1)
  for (pk in peaks) {
    idx <- which.min(abs(pos - pk$center))
    offsets <- seq(-floor(length(pk$vals)/2), floor(length(pk$vals)/2),
                   length.out = length(pk$vals))
    pval[idx + offsets] <- pk$vals
  }
  data.frame(CHROM = chr_label, position = pos, value = pval)
}

# ── Chr 1: 3 loci, one with sub-peaks close together ───────────────────────────
chr1 <- make_chr("1", n = 500, max_bp = 5e6, peaks = list(
  list(center = 1050000, vals = c(1e-5, 1e-6, 1e-7, 1e-10, 1e-8, 1e-6, 1e-5)),
  list(center = 2500000, vals = c(1e-6, 1e-7, 3e-9, 1e-7, 1e-6)),
  list(center = 4000000, vals = c(1e-6, 2e-8, 1e-6, 1e-6, 5e-9, 1e-6))
))

# ── Chr 2: 2 loci, one very sharp, one broad ───────────────────────────────────
chr2 <- make_chr("2", n = 400, max_bp = 4e6, peaks = list(
  list(center = 800000,  vals = c(1e-5, 1e-12, 1e-5)),
  list(center = 3200000, vals = c(1e-5, 1e-6, 1e-7, 1e-8, 1e-7, 1e-6, 1e-5))
))

# ── Chr 3: 1 borderline locus ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
chr3 <- make_chr("3", n = 300, max_bp = 3e6, peaks = list(
  list(center = 1500000, vals = c(4e-8, 6e-8, 4e-8))
))

# ── Chr 4: no significant loci (null chromosome) ──────────────────────────────
chr4 <- make_chr("4", n = 200, max_bp = 2e6, peaks = list())

# ── Combine ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
toy_multi <- do.call(rbind, list(chr1, chr2, chr3, chr4))
rownames(toy_multi) <- NULL

blocks <- physical_merge(toy_multi, sig_th = 5e-8, window = 500000, reward = "min")
blocks
```
